description: An ancient Byzantine icon depicting the Virgin Mary in the Hagiosoritissa (Intercessor) type. Mary is shown in a three-quarter view facing right, her hands raised together in a gesture of prayer and supplication. She wears a dark maphorion (veil) over her head and shoulders, with visible gold striations from the aged surface. Her face is rendered with large, expressive dark eyes and a solemn expression. The background is gold leaf, heavily worn and cracked with age. The icon panel shows significant patina and surface deterioration consistent with great antiquity.
theological_description: The Hagiosoritissa iconographic type presents the Virgin Mary as the supreme intercessor, standing in prayer before God on behalf of humanity. Without the Christ Child present, the composition emphasizes Mary's role as advocate and mediator of grace. Her prayerful gesture reflects her perpetual intercession described in Christian tradition. The image draws on Luke's portrayal of Mary as the faithful handmaid of the Lord who pondered divine mysteries in her heart, and on the broader scriptural tradition of the righteous who pray ceaselessly for others.
